rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrocatalytic dehydrogenative carbocyclization of unactivated alkene-tethered quinazolin-4-ones with diethyl malonate to give polycyclic pyrroloquinazolinones, with H2 as the sole byproduct.
electrochemical_role: Anodic PCET oxidation of base-activated diethyl malonate to the malonate radical; cathodic reduction of protons to H2; the Ir(ppy)3 oxidative quenching photocatalytic cycle anchors and oxidizes the cyclized carbon radical intermediate (IrIII* oxidizes C to nitranion D, regenerated IrII reduced back to IrIII at the anode/by anodic events).

key_intermediates.name: malonate radical (deprotonated diethyl malonate, one-electron oxidized) - intermediate A
alkyl radical B from addition of malonate radical onto tethered alkene of 1a
N-centered (amidinyl) cyclized radical C after intramolecular radical trapping by amidine N
nitranion intermediate D (from SET oxidation of C by excited IrIII*)
tetrahydropyrroloquinazolinone E (protonated D, detected by HRMS)
pyrroloquinazolinone product 3a (after anodic oxidation of E)

electron_flow: Base-activated diethyl malonate undergoes anodic PCET to give a malonate radical, which adds to the tethered alkene of 1a to form alkyl radical B; intramolecular cyclization onto the amidine N gives radical C, which is oxidized by photoexcited IrIII* to nitranion D (closing the oxidative-quenching photocycle, with IrII reoxidized to IrIII). Protonation of D yields E, anodically oxidized/deprotonated to product 3a, while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed (0% 3a), consistent with radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Malonate-DPE adduct 5 isolated in 66%, indicating involvement of malonate radical

probe_reagents.name: Pre-generated IrIV / IrCl4 (stoichiometric oxidant, no current)
probe_reagents.observation: No 3a formed, ruling out a discrete IrIV oxidant pathway (path c)

probe_reagents.name: UVA irradiation without PC
probe_reagents.observation: No beneficial effect (35-38% vs 36% dark), excluding triplet energy transfer sensitization of 1b (path d)

probe_reagents.name: On/off light and current experiments
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction halts when either light or current is off, ruling out a radical chain

probe_reagents.name: Hydrogen detection
probe_reagents.observation: H2 confirmed at cathode, consistent with proposed cathodic proton reduction

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, 3a, Ir(ppy)3
probe_reagents.observation: E_p/2 values established: 2a-base 0.83 V, 1a 1.92 V, 3a -0.72 V vs SCE; IrIII* reduction potential 0.31 V; supports anodic malonate oxidation and unfavorable SET from C to IrIII* being thermodynamically uphill only when product 3a (-1.85 V vs SCE for IrII/IrIII).

notes: Authors explicitly disfavor alternative paths: (path a) merged electrolysis + reductive quenching Ir cycle - excluded by Stern-Volmer (2a does not quench IrIII*) and failure of stoichiometric Ir(ppy)3 photooxidant without current; (path b) IrIV electron-shuttle - excluded by failure of electrogenerated IrIV and IrCl4 to drive the reaction without current; (path c) excited IrIV oxidative pathway and (path d) triplet sensitization of quinazolinone 1b - excluded by UVA experiments without PC; (path e) reductive-quenching SET from C to IrIII* - excluded as thermodynamically unfavorable. Favored mechanism is the oxidative-quenching photocatalytic cycle (paths f/g) that anchors and rapidly oxidizes the anodically generated radical intermediate C. Hydrogen evolution at cathode maintains electron balance.
